Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface Configuration

All Known Subinterfaces:
GuavaRequirements, JavaRequirements
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultConfiguration, DefaultGuavaRequirements, DefaultJavaRequirements, Requirements
public interface Configuration
Configures the behavior of a single Requirements instance.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    assertionsAreEnabled()
    Indicates if assertThat() will verify requirements; otherwise, it won't do anything.
    Configuration
    copy()
    Returns a copy of this configuration.
    Map<String,Object>
    getContext()
    Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.
    boolean
    isCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates if stack trace references to this library should be removed, so long as it does not result in any user code being removed.
    boolean
    isDiffEnabled()
    Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    String
    toString(Object value)
    Returns the String representation of an object.
    Configuration
    withAssertionsDisabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
    Configuration
    withAssertionsEnabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() should verify requirements.
    Configuration
    withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be removed, so long as it does not result in any user code being removed.
    Configuration
    withConfiguration(Configuration configuration)
    Replaces the configuration.
    Configuration
    withContext(String name, Object value)
    Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message.
    Configuration
    withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    Configuration
    withoutAnyContext()
    Removes all contextual information associated with the exception message.
    Configuration
    withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be kept.
    Configuration
    withoutContext(String name)
    Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
    Configuration
    withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <T> Configuration
    withoutStringConverter(Class<T> type)
    Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
    <T> Configuration
    withStringConverter(Class<T> type, Function<T,String> converter)
    Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

  • Method Details

    • copy

      Configuration copy()
      Returns a copy of this configuration. This method is thread-safe. Other methods may not be.
      Returns:
      a copy of this configuration

    • assertionsAreEnabled

      boolean assertionsAreEnabled()
      Indicates if assertThat() will verify requirements; otherwise, it won't do anything.
      Returns:
      true if assertions are enabled for this class

    • withAssertionsEnabled

      Configuration withAssertionsEnabled()
      Indicates that assertThat() should verify requirements.
      Returns:
      this

    • withAssertionsDisabled

      Configuration withAssertionsDisabled()
      Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
      Returns:
      this

    • isDiffEnabled

      boolean isDiffEnabled()
      Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      true by default

    • withDiff

      Configuration withDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      this

    • withoutDiff

      Configuration withoutDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      this

    • isCleanStackTrace

      boolean isCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates if stack trace references to this library should be removed, so long as it does not result in any user code being removed.
      Returns:
      true by default
      See Also:
      • withCleanStackTrace()
      • withoutCleanStackTrace()

    • withCleanStackTrace

      Configuration withCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be removed, so long as it does not result in any user code being removed.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      • isCleanStackTrace()

    • withoutCleanStackTrace

      Configuration withoutCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be kept.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      • isCleanStackTrace()

    • getContext

      Map<String,Object> getContext()
      Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.
      Returns:
      an empty map by default
      See Also:
      • withContext(String, Object)

    • withContext

      Configuration withContext(String name, Object value)
      Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message. Overrides any values associated with the name at the ThreadRequirements level.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      value - the value of the parameter
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null

    • withoutContext

      Configuration withoutContext(String name)
      Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null

    • withoutAnyContext

      Configuration withoutAnyContext()
      Removes all contextual information associated with the exception message.
      Returns:
      this

    • toString

      String toString(Object value)
      Returns the String representation of an object. By default, custom handlers are provided for arrays, Integer, Long, BigDecimal, List, Set, Map, Path, and Throwable.
      Parameters:
      value - a value
      Returns:
      the String representation of the value
      See Also:
      • withStringConverter(Class, Function)

    • withStringConverter

      <T> Configuration withStringConverter(Class<T> type, Function<T,String> converter)
      Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      Please note that type must be an exact match (e.g. configuring a converter for Set will not match values of type HashSet).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted (non-primitive arrays are mapped to Object[].class)
      converter - a function that converts an object of the specified type to a String
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any of the arguments are null

    • withoutStringConverter

      <T> Configuration withoutStringConverter(Class<T> type)
      Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

    • withConfiguration

      Configuration withConfiguration(Configuration configuration)
      Replaces the configuration.
      Parameters:
      configuration - a new configuration
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if configuration is null
